vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-982: Evaluate Claude Code --agent/subagent system for agent lifecycle

plan.title: Claude Code --agent integration for agent lifecycle
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Panopticon generates complex bash launcher scripts (launcher-generator.ts) that hardcode model selection, permission flags, and tool access for each agent type. Claude Code v2.1.117+ provides a native agent definition system (.claude/agents/*.md) that handles model, permissions, tools, and per-agent hooks declaratively. The current approach is fragile (shell quoting, env var ordering) and duplicates what Claude Code now handles natively.
narratives.Proposal: Define 7 Panopticon pipeline agent types as .claude/agents/pan-*.md files. Modify getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand() and generateLauncherScript() to emit --agent pan-<type> instead of inline --model/--permission-mode flags. Migrate PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop hooks from global settings.json into per-agent frontmatter. Launcher.sh shrinks to env-only (provider routing, Panopticon identity, terminal setup). Non-Anthropic model routing and non-frontmatter hook events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, etc.) remain in launcher/global hooks.

narrative.Action: Create .claude/agents/pan-work-agent.md with YAML frontmatter: model sonnet, permissionMode bypassPermissions, all tools, effort high. Include PreToolUse (pre-tool-hook, tldr-read-enforcer), PostToolUse (heartbeat-hook, permission-event-hook, inspect-on-bead-close, tldr-post-edit), and Stop (stop-hook, permission-event-hook) hooks. Markdown body describes autonomous implementation role.

subItems.title: pan-work-agent.md exists in .claude/agents/ with correct YAML frontmatter (model, permissionMode, tools, hooks)
claude --agent pan-work-agent --print runs on sonnet with bypassPermissions (verified via --output-format json)

narrative.Action: Create .claude/agents/pan-planning-agent.md with YAML frontmatter: model sonnet, permissionMode bypassPermissions, all tools, effort high. Hooks: PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop as appropriate for planning (no inspect-on-bead-close, no caveman). Markdown body describes planning-only role (no implementation, vBRIEF + continue.json output).

subItems.title: pan-planning-agent.md exists with correct frontmatter; does NOT include inspect-on-bead-close hook
claude --agent pan-planning-agent --print runs on sonnet (verified via --output-format json)

narrative.Action: Create .claude/agents/pan-review-agent.md with YAML frontmatter: model opus, permissionMode plan, tools restricted to read-only (Read, Grep, Glob, Bash). Hooks: heartbeat + stop only (no pre-tool-hook, no tldr, no inspect). Markdown body describes strict code review role.

subItems.title: pan-review-agent.md exists with tools restricted to Read, Grep, Glob, Bash and permissionMode plan
claude --agent pan-review-agent --print runs on opus with plan permissions (verified via --output-format json)

narrative.Action: Create .claude/agents/pan-test-agent.md: model sonnet, permissionMode bypassPermissions, all tools. Hooks: heartbeat + stop. Markdown body describes test execution and failure analysis role.

subItems.title: pan-test-agent.md exists with correct frontmatter and test-execution role description

narrative.Action: Create .claude/agents/pan-inspect-agent.md: model sonnet, permissionMode plan, tools restricted to read-only. No caveman hooks (inspect sentinels must not be compressed). Markdown body describes per-bead spec verification role.

subItems.title: pan-inspect-agent.md exists with plan permission mode and NO caveman hooks

narrative.Action: Create .claude/agents/pan-uat-agent.md: model sonnet, permissionMode bypassPermissions, all tools plus Playwright MCP server configured. Markdown body describes browser-based requirement verification role.

subItems.title: pan-uat-agent.md exists with Playwright MCP server configured in frontmatter

narrative.Action: Create .claude/agents/pan-merge-agent.md: model sonnet, permissionMode bypassPermissions, all tools. Hooks: heartbeat + stop. Markdown body describes PR merge, conflict resolution, and post-merge cleanup role.

subItems.title: pan-merge-agent.md exists with correct frontmatter and merge-role description

narrative.Action: Modify getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand() in src/lib/agents.ts to accept an agentType parameter and emit 'claude --agent pan-<type>' instead of 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --permission-mode bypassPermissions --model <model>'. For Anthropic models, omit --model (frontmatter handles it). For non-Anthropic models, add --model override alongside --agent. The claudish path remains unchanged (non-direct providers still need the wrapper).

subItems.title: getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand() accepts agentType and returns 'claude --agent pan-<type>' for Anthropic models
For non-Anthropic direct providers, command includes both --agent and --model override
Claudish (non-direct) provider path unchanged — still uses claudish wrapper

items.title: Remove permission flags from LauncherConfig and buildCommand

narrative.Action: Update generateLauncherScript() and buildNonConversationCommand() in launcher-generator.ts to stop emitting --dangerously-skip-permissions and --permission-mode flags when --agent is used. Permission mode is now in agent frontmatter. Keep permissionFlags for conversation agents and specialist-dispatch (which may not use --agent). Remove the token-level dedup logic for permission flags from buildNonConversationCommand() where --agent makes it redundant.

subItems.title: Generated launcher scripts for work/planning/resume agents no longer contain --dangerously-skip-permissions or --permission-mode when --agent is present
Conversation agent and specialist-dispatch launcher paths still work (they may not use --agent)

items.title: Update spawnAgent() to pass agentType through launcher config

narrative.Action: Update spawnAgent() in src/lib/agents.ts to pass the Panopticon agent type (work, planning, review, etc.) to getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand(). Update all 4 callsites of generateLauncherScript() in agents.ts: line 1092 (work agent), line 1444 (fallback/recovery), line 1656 (resume). Ensure the agentType maps correctly to the pan-<type>-agent.md filename.

subItems.title: spawnAgent() for work agents passes agentType='work' to getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand()
Resume path (line 1656) uses --agent pan-work-agent alongside --resume
Fallback/recovery path (line 1444) uses correct agent type

items.title: Update specialist dispatch to use --agent for specialist types

narrative.Action: Find specialist dispatch code (likely in src/lib/cloister/) that spawns review, test, inspect, uat, and merge agents. Update their launcher configuration to use --agent pan-<type>-agent. Specialist dispatch uses script -qfaec wrapper and specialist-dispatch agentType — verify --agent flag passes through the wrapper correctly.

subItems.title: Review specialist spawns with --agent pan-review-agent
Test, inspect, uat, merge specialists each use their corresponding pan-*-agent definition
script -qfaec wrapper correctly passes --agent flag to inner Claude invocation

items.title: Move PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop hooks from global settings to per-agent frontmatter

narrative.Action: Remove PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop hook entries from the global ~/.claude/settings.json that are now defined in per-agent frontmatter. This must happen atomically with agent definition deployment to avoid duplicate firing. The pan sync command should handle this — when it deploys agent definitions, it should also prune the global hooks that are now per-agent. Keep SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification, PermissionRequest hooks in global settings (they can't move to frontmatter).

subItems.title: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop hooks removed from global settings.json
Global settings.json retains SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification, PermissionRequest hooks
User's ad-hoc Claude sessions (no --agent) still get global hooks for non-migrated events
No hook fires twice (global + per-agent) for any event type

narrative.Action: Update tests in tests/lib/launcher-generator.test.ts to verify: (1) --agent flag present in generated scripts for work/planning/resume types, (2) --model omitted for Anthropic models when --agent is used, (3) --model present alongside --agent for non-Anthropic models, (4) permission flags absent when --agent handles permissions, (5) conversation and specialist-dispatch paths still work without --agent if needed.

subItems.title: Tests verify --agent flag in generated launcher scripts for work, planning, resume agent types
Tests verify --model omission for Anthropic models when --agent is active
Tests verify permission flags absent when --agent is active

items.title: Update pan sync to deploy agent definition files

narrative.Action: Update the pan sync command (src/lib/sync.ts) to ensure pan-*.md agent definitions are deployed to project .claude/agents/ directories. Currently sync handles skills and settings — extend it to handle agent definitions. The definitions live in the panopticon-cli repo and need to be available in each managed project's workspace.

subItems.title: pan sync copies pan-*.md agent definitions to project .claude/agents/ directories
Existing non-pan-* agent definitions in .claude/agents/ are preserved (not deleted)

items.title: Add --name flag to agent spawn for human-readable session names

narrative.Action: Add --name agent-pan-<issueId> to the launcher command construction in getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand() or spawnAgent(). This creates human-readable Claude session names that are discoverable via claude --resume. Optional enhancement — not load-bearing for the migration.

subItems.title: Spawned agents have --name <agentId> in their launch command
claude --resume <agentId> can find and resume the session by name
